target.name: Dominion Dynamics

methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

private.name: Tenna Systems

data.narrative:
Dominion Dynamics is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Eliot Pence (former Anduril International leader, 2018–2022) and Mitch Carkner (ex-Amazon, IBM, Canadian Forces). The company has raised $18M to date. The peer universe comprises 10 comparable firms: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private players including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression, using the $18M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage transitions, implies a valuation of $45M. Peer-Median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, Ares Industries at $150M, NODA AI at $125M, and Tenna Systems at $60M) yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $38M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $98M. The stage-progression floor reflects confidence in capital-raised fundamentals; the peer-median ceiling incorporates market-comparable transactions in an active frontier-defense sector. Key uncertainties include product-market fit maturity, near-term revenue trajectory, and customer concentration—typical unknowns at Seed stage that will narrow materially upon Series A fundraising.
